events.title: Canada police investigate whether Toronto police death linked to global terror attacks
events.description: Constable Marc Pinizzotto, 43, was killed while executing search warrants related to a shooting at US consulate Authorities in Canada are investigating whether the killing of a Toronto police officer while he was executing search warrants related to a shooting at the city’s US consulate is linked a broader series of global terror attacks. Constable Marc Pinizzotto, 43, a member of the emergency taskforce, was killed on Thursday during a dawn search of an apartment building in the west of the city. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Canada police investigate whether Toronto police death linked to global terror attacks

events.title: Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout
events.description: Three Indian seafarers were killed in an attack on an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday, as renewed hostilities in one of the world’s most critical shipping corridors once again heightened concern over food security, fuel prices and broken global supply chains.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Three seafarers killed in Hormuz strike as UN warns of widening fallout

events.title: Deadly Philippines earthquake found to have raised seabed by up to 2 metres
events.description: ‘Coastal uplift’ exposes coral and kills marine life, as residents say shorelines extended by up to 200 metres A powerful earthquake that killed at least 61 people in the Philippines this week raised the seabed by as much as 2 metres (6.6ft), exposing coral and harming marine life, the country’s environment department has said. At least 40 people are still missing after the 7.8-magnitude quake off southern Mindanao island on Monday, according to updated tolls from the disaster agency. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Deadly Philippines earthquake found to have raised seabed by up to 2 metres

events.title: World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe

events.ticker_label: 💥 World News in Brief: Turk condemns Northern Ireland riots, jobs at risk over animal disease spread, heatwaves spark public health crisis in Europe

events.title: Middle East LIVE: Diplomacy in focus as escalation ‘reverberates across borders and continents’, warns UN chief
events.description: Nearly four months after the latest Middle East crisis erupted and despite a fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, tensions continue to reverberate across the region and beyond. The UN Security Council held a high-level debate on advancing political solutions in the Middle East on Wednesday, amid continuing conflicts, humanitarian emergencies and concerns over regional stability. Follow live in-depth meetings coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East LIVE: Diplomacy in focus as escalation ‘reverberates across borders and continents’, warns UN chief

events.title: Trump says leader of Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang killed in US strike
events.description: President says Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores killed in ‘swift and lethal’ military strike with help from Venezuela The US military has killed a leader in the Venezuelan street gang Tren de Aragua, Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, with the help of Venezuela, Donald Trump announced on Friday. “At my direction, the United States Southern Command delivered a swift and lethal kinetic strike to successfully execute Niño Guerrero, the infamous leader of Tren De Aragua, one of the most bloodthirsty Terrorist Organizations on Planet Earth,” Trump wrote on Truth Social. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Trump says leader of Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang killed in US strike

events.title: Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks
events.description: A Guardian analysis reveals how most of 39 countries facing US entry restrictions are most vulnerable environmentally ‘Every day it’s more barriers’: how the US is shutting out climate refugees Donald Trump ’s immigration crackdown is largely targeting people from the countries most vulnerable to displacement from climate-driven disasters, a Guardian analysis shows. As the Trump administration pushes policies to boost planet-heating fossil fuels , millions of people are being forced to flee their homelands due to storms, floods and droughts worsened by the climate crisis . Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump targeting immigrants from countries hit most by climate shocks

events.title: ‘Spy turtles’ and ‘spy fish’ being used to monitor Chinese waters, Beijing claims
events.description: Ministry says animals fitted with sensors by foreign agencies collect sensitive sea data, in ‘invisible secret war’ China’s ministry of state security has claimed that foreign espionage and intelligence agencies are using innovative new methods to monitor the country’s waters, including deploying “spy” animals fitted with sensors. In a post on the Chinese platform WeChat on Friday, the ministry warned that an “invisible secret war” was quietly playing out in the seas around China as foreign agencies were collecting sensitive data “through a variety of new spying devices” to produce underwater maps that pose a “serious threat to our national security”. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Spy turtles’ and ‘spy fish’ being used to monitor Chinese waters, Beijing claims

events.title: Australian girl killed in Pakistan after reportedly being shot by police
events.description: Nine-year-old visiting relatives in Punjab province when police opened fire on car, local media report Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A nine-year-old Australian girl has been killed and two of her family members injured after reportedly being shot by police in Pakistan. The family were visiting a relative in Chakwal, in Punjab province, when they were robbed while in their rental car on Wednesday night local time, Pakistani English-language news outlet Dawn reported . Continue reading...

events.title: Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever
events.description: From trade wars to soaring ticket prices, the 2026 World Cup is unlike any before it. Faisal Islam explores what this tournament reveals about our changing global economy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why the economics make this the craziest World Cup ever

events.title: NSW lifts drone ban over Sydney beach after shark attack – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Senate will deliver its report from the NDIS inquiry on Tuesday. Butler doesn’t directly answer a question about whether or not he is willing to make any significant changes to the legislation the government has put forward. He says: Look, the direction of travel I think is an important one for us to follow through. This largely reflects reviews and insights that we’ve gathered over the last three years since the independent review was done of the NDIS in 2023 and endorsed by all governments at national Cabinet later that year. I’m utterly convinced this is the right plan for the NDIS… I’m not saying there won’t be any change. We heard ideas from the crossbench in the debate in the House of Representatives. I’ve very much heard that people want greater reassurance about what won’t change because of the reforms we’re putting in place. We have to constrain that growth. Without reforms it would grow to $20 billion by the end of the decade and that’s simply not a sustainable position. It’s also allowed us, as I said, to protect the core supports people rely upon for that safety that was part of your introduction. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 NSW lifts drone ban over Sydney beach after shark attack – as it happened

events.title: ‘There was a lot of blood in the water’: paddleboarder rescues woman after ‘shocking’ Coogee shark attack
events.description: Charlie Verco managed to grab hold of the woman and bring her back to shore after the Sydney shark attack on Saturday Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Elite paddleboarder Charlie Verco has only seen one shark bigger than the one he saw on Saturday at Sydney’s Coogee beach. The North Bondi athlete was training for July’s world championships in Hawaii on Saturday morning when he heard a swimmer shouting “shark”. Continue reading...

events.title: Women and girls caught up in Yemen’s ‘forgotten crisis’ bear the heaviest toll as funding falls
events.description: Yemen remains gripped by one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises, with 22 million – out of a population of 35 million – requiring assistance. Women and girls account for half of those in need, and two-thirds of them are of childbearing age, placing reproductive health at the heart of the emergency.

events.title: Angus Taylor using NDIS cuts as ‘pawn in bigger chess game’, Mark Butler warns
events.description: Opposition and Greens could team up to extend inquiry into changes but health minister ‘utterly convinced’ federal government’s plan is right Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Mark Butler has doubled down on his plans to pass contentious NDIS changes as early as this month, warning the opposition against using the bill as a “pawn in a bigger chess game” as they consider a potential deal with the Greens. The Coalition and the Greens have criticised Labor’s truncated timeline to pass NDIS, negative gearing and capital gains changes, and have signalled they could team up against the government in the Senate to extend inquiries into the bills. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Angus Taylor using NDIS cuts as ‘pawn in bigger chess game’, Mark Butler warns

events.title: Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds
events.description: Amazon and Sony among firms that may have sourced coltan, used in phones, from supply chains controlled by the M23 rebels, says Global Witness Leading global brands including Amazon, Ericsson and Sony are “likely” to have sourced minerals linked to a militia accused of widespread sexual violence, summary executions and torture, a new investigation claims. The companies allegedly, but unknowingly, acquired coltan smuggled from mines in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) that are occupied by the M23 militia , which has committed myriad atrocities in eastern DRC. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Global brands ‘likely’ using mineral that funds rebels accused of atrocities in DRC, investigation finds

events.title: Jessie J’s triumphant return puts lucrative Chinese market in spotlight
events.description: Other western acts have attempted to crack country’s music scene since singer’s breakout success in 2018 One week after announcing she was “cancer free”, the British pop star Jessie J did what any recovering patient would do and travelled thousands of miles around the world to perform for an audience of more than a billion people. On 29 May, the singer-songwriter, whose real name is Jessica Cornish, belted out a stage-rattling rendition of Frank Sinatra’s My Way on the stage of Singer, a hugely popular Chinese singing competition similar to The Voice. She also performed her new song, California, briefly adapting the lyrics to change California to Changsha, the Chinese city where Singer is hosted. Continue reading...

events.title: WHO report shows progress in blood safety, but there are worrying gaps
events.description: Every day, safe blood helps save the lives of women experiencing childbirth complications, accident victims, cancer patients and people living with chronic diseases. Yet despite decades of progress, access to lifesaving blood remains deeply unequal, with shortages continuing to put lives at risk in many lower-income countries, according to a new World Health Organization (WHO) report.

Since December 2022, Princess Bajrakitiyabha had been in hospital, having collapsed while out training her dogs. After nearly four years in a coma, the princess died earlier this week . events.title: Trump at 80: "Harry Houdini" & A Cage Fight
events.description: President Trump turns 80 today, and the White House lawn has been transformed into a giant arena, complete with floodlights, cameras, giant Star-Spangled Banners and a 90-foot scaffold known as “The Claw,” as 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue hosts “Freedom 250”, a celebration featuring professional UFC cage fighting. The event combines three occasions: President Trump’s birthday, Flag Day, which marks the adoption of the U.S. flag in 1777, and the launch of celebrations leading up to the 250th anniversary of American independence. To discuss the significance of the event, France 24's Gavin Lee is joined by Jeffrey Sonnenfeld, author of Trump’s Ten Commandments and Senior Associate Dean for Leadership Studies at the Yale School of Management. He has advised five U.S. presidents and has known President Trump for more than 25 years.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Trump at 80: "Harry Houdini" & A Cage Fight

events.title: Switzerland rejects a cap on its population in a referendum
events.description: Switzerland has rejected a cap on the country's population when the proposal was put to the vote in a nationwide referendum.

events.title: Ukrainian national who completed Air Force officer training convicted in ghost gun 3D printing operation
events.description: Yaroslav Vishnevski convicted on five federal counts after authorities found ghost gun 3D printing operation and illegal silencers from China.

events.title: Italy: Thousands attend anti- and pro-migration rallies in Rome

events.title: The week in pictures: World Cup 2026 kicks off, the Pope visits Spain and the White House prepares for a cage fight showdown
events.description: The Pope visits Segrada Familia during his trip to Barcelona, the 2026 football World Cup kicks off in Mexico city, and the White House prepares for its first cage fight showdown. FRANCE 24 looks back at some of the week's most striking images.

events.title: The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
events.description: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

events.title: Jamaica’s beach access crisis: ‘We shouldn’t be forced to fight for what is already ours’
events.description: Activists argue business model is ‘plantation tourism’ designed to benefit elite and disadvantage most Jamaicans Campaigners go to court to fight privatisation of Jamaican coast Devon Taylor remembers when the Mammee Bay shoreline in St Ann, Jamaica, was filled with children frolicking in the ocean after school, fishers haggling with locals over the price of their daily catch and craft vendors carving souvenirs under almond trees. “I grew up on Mammee Bay,” Taylor says. He recalls fetching seawater in bottles for his grandmother when she was no longer able to go to the beach, learning to swim in the shallows, and watching generations of fishers cast their nets. “That beach raised us. It fed us.” Continue reading...

events.title: Police fire tear gas at anti-G7 protesters in Geneva
events.description: Protesters in Geneva clashed with police, set a car on fire and smashed the windows of a bank to show their opposition to the G7 summit taking place across the border in France.

events.title: Fact check: These FIFA World Cup ticket sites are fake
events.description: Desperate to get hold of tickets for the football World Cup, many fans face being scammed by sites set up to sell fake tickets. Here's how to make sure you don't fall into the trap.

events.title: Swiss police fire tear gas as anti-G7 protesters target UN buildings
events.description: Swiss police on Sunday fired tear gas at demonstrators protesting the upcoming Group of Seven summit in France after some protesters started targeting buildings linked to the United Nations in Geneva.

events.title: Brazil struggle against Morocco, Australia stun Turkiye
events.description: Morocco looked impressive in their first game against Brazil, but Vinicius managed to bring it level with a beautiful goal. Switzerland also struggled against Qatar, despite being favourites to top their group. Scotland brought the vibes to their match against Haiti, and a deflected shot by McGuinn gave Scotland their first world cup win in 28 years. Unexpectedly, Australia won 2-0 against Turkiye.

events.title: Pope Leo hits beaches of popular European migrant entry point after criticizing global immigration policies

events.title: Mother hopes to get justice for murdered daughter in retrial in Dominican Republic
events.description: Shirley Firth is hoping those responsible for Lindsay de Feliz’s death in 2019 will finally be convicted A Cambridgeshire mother in her 90s is hoping to finally see justice for her murdered daughter when a retrial into her death is due to open in the Dominican Republic this week. The body of Lindsay de Feliz, 64, a successful author, was found in a shallow grave, close to her home in the north-west of the Dominican Republic, in December 2019. Continue reading...

events.title: Jamaican beach access campaigners go to court to fight privatisation of coast
events.description: Activists are challenging colonial-era law and demanding ‘free, legal, unfettered, forever rights’ to use beaches Jamaica’s beach access crisis: ‘We shouldn’t be forced to fight for what is already ours’ Campaigners in Jamaica are heading to court next week to try to prevent the government from cutting off access to more of their beaches. They argue that ceding their shorelines to big hotel chains enriches private investors and benefits tourists and outsiders while depriving Jamaicans who depend on the sea for their livelihoods, leisure and health. Continue reading...

events.title: Trump hosts White House cage fights to mark 80th birthday
events.description: The US president is set to watch seven fights with an audience of 4,000 people inside a specially built arena on the White House lawn. Critics say the unprecendented spectacle is in poor taste amid the ongoing Iran war.

We discuss it with Ellen Kountz, Dean of the Finance Department at INSEEC Business School.

events.title: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
events.description: Police dispersed demonstrators in Nanyuki, 120 miles from Nairobi, amid rising anger at US plans Kenyan police have shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed Ebola quarantine facility for US citizens. Patrick Wahome, who has organised protests in Nanyuki against the centre, told Reuters on Tuesday the man died from a gunshot wound to the head. Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Bandits in north-west Nigeria abduct villagers they invited to discuss peace talks
events.description: Thirty-nine people taken near Magamin Diddi village in Maradun municipality, north-west Zamfara state, police say Armed bandits in north-west Nigeria abducted dozens of villagers whom they invited to a meeting about potential peace negotiations, authorities and residents said on Monday, highlighting the region’s worsening security. According to local police, 39 people were seized on Sunday during a meeting in the forest near Magamin Diddi village in the Maradun municipality of north-west Zamfara state. But some residents and officials believe the number of those abducted could be as high as 50. Continue reading...

events.title: China arrests US academic at conference for ‘espionage activities’
events.description: Arrest of Min Zin, who writes about Myanmar and Chinese foreign policy, comes just month after Trump visit to Beijing China has arrested a US scholar who writes about Myanmar and Chinese foreign policy on suspicion of spying. Min Zin was suspected of “engaging in espionage activities that endanger China’s national security,” China’s ministry of foreign affairs spokesperson, Lin Jian, said on Friday. Continue reading...

events.title: China arrests a U.S. scholar with a history of Myanmar activism, suspected of spying
events.description: China's government said Min Zin, who heads a think tank focused on Myanmar, was detained on suspicion of engaging in "espionage and endangering Chinese national security."

events.title: Nigeria: Abducted former general dies in captivity

events.title: 'Must-see event', rare sumo tournament kicks off in Paris
events.description: A 150-strong delegation, two chartered planes... More than 30 years after their last visit to France, the elite of sumo wrestling – Japan's national and sacred sport – is making a highly anticipated return to Paris for a weekend showcase.

events.title: Switzerland: After rapid population growth, right-wing party supports cap vote

events.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
events.description: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

events.title: Why is Bulgaria banning arms supplies to Ukraine?
events.description: Bulgaria's new government has banned state arms supplies to Ukraine. The ban does not, however, affect commercial exports, raising the question as to whether the move was aimed more at a domestic audience than at Kyiv.

events.title: The creatives trying to fix Germany's bureaucracy
events.description: Berlin played host to the 9th annual Creative Bureaucracy Festival this week, where highly-motivated public service workers explained why a healthy democracy needs a functioning state.

events.title: Mexican authorities investigate homicide of mayor in Oaxaca state
events.description: Mexican authorities are investigating the homicide of a mayor in San Miguel Amatitlan, Oaxaca

events.title: ‘Grave concern’ after dozens of women arrested in Afghanistan for dress violations
events.description: Gender equality agency UN Women is “gravely concerned” by the arrest of at least 30 women in Herat city last weekend in Afghanistan for allegedly violating dress requirements imposed by Taliban authorities.

events.title: Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump’s Iran pressure campaign after regional trip: ‘Zero daylight’
events.description: Ambassador Mike Waltz says Gulf allies back Trump's blockade and economic pressure campaign against Iran, with UAE and Bahrain validating U.S. strategy.

events.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
events.description: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

events.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
events.description: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

events.title: An exiled Russian journalist starts new career as a comedian

events.title: Pope Leo XIV's flight home from Spain was grounded so the king came to his aid
events.description: Leo's Iberia charter, due to take him back to Rome after a weeklong visit to Spain, was grounded by a technical problem Friday, prompting Spain's king to offer his private jet instead.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly

events.title: Liberal frontbencher Jonno Duniam to quit politics, says leadership spill ‘started to really wear on me’
events.description: Tasmanian senator says he feels he is ‘letting down the team’ but needs to prioritise family Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Liberal frontbencher Jonno Duniam will quit politics before the end of the year, in another major blow to the struggling Coalition as it experiences its lowest levels of popularity in decades. The Tasmanian senator, who was elected to parliament in 2016 and is seen as one of the Liberals’ best talents, said it was an “extremely difficult decision to make” but one he had been considering for “quite some time” to spend more time with family. Continue reading...

events.title: Sahel juntas are 'crushing' basic freedoms
events.description: In the Sahel countries of Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger, military rulers are increasingly clamping down on press freedom and freedom of expression. Many journalists, bloggers and activists have been forced into exile.

events.title: New York Knicks win first NBA championship in 53 years
events.description: Series MVP Jalen Brunson has led the New York Knicks to another comeback victory over the San Antonio Spurs. The win secured the franchise's third NBA championship, and its first since 1973.

events.title: US deports migrants, including Iranian activist, to CAR
events.description: The number of migrants deported is not yet clear, but it is believed to be around two dozen. The Trump administration has signed controversial deals with several African countries to deport migrants it cannot send home.

events.title: Tech 24 takes to the skies as VivaTech takes over the Champs-Elysées
events.description: France’s biggest geek get-together started with a bang this year. For its 10th anniversary, the VivaTech trade show took to the Champs-Elysées, billing the installation as "Europe's largest open-air technology experience."

events.title: UK intercepts Russian shadow fleet vessel in Channel: ministry

events.title: UK celebrates King Charles’s official birthday with Trooping the Colour
events.description: Crowds have gathered in central London for the Trooping the Colour ceremony to mark King Charles III’s official birthday.

events.title: EU says to resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday
events.description: The European Union will resume membership talks with Ukraine on Monday, the bloc announced, after Hungary lifted the veto imposed by its previous pro-Russian leader.

events.title: G7 meeting begins in France this Monday
events.description: U.S. ​President ​Donald Trump will meet with Middle ​Eastern ‌leaders ⁠and ‌attend a working session ⁠with Ukrainian President ​Volodymyr Zelenskiy ‌during the G7 summit ‌in France ​next week, senior U.S. administration officials ​said.

events.title: US: New York Knicks win first NBA championship since 1973
events.description: The New York Knicks are champions of the NBA for the first time since 1973, beating the San Antonio Spurs in five games for this title. The clincher came Saturday night in a 94-90 victory, the Knicks' fourth comeback win of the series.

events.title: Colombia leftist candidate says ready to overhaul peace process
events.description: Colombia's left-wing presidential hopeful Ivan Cepeda said he was open to making changes to the much-criticized peace process launched by outgoing President Gustavo Petro, his staunchest ally.

events.title: British 1-2-3 at Barcelona-Catalunya Grand Prix - first since 1968
events.description: Sir Lewis Hamilton has won his first Formula 1 Grand Prix victory with Ferrari, while George Russell and defending champion Lando Norris made it the first all-British podium since 1968.

events.title: 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies climbing volcanic crater
events.description: A daredevil adventurer nicknamed "The Spider-Man of Yemen" has died after falling into a volcano crater while attempting to climb a vertical rock face without safety equipment.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ 'Spider-Man of Yemen' dies climbing volcanic crater

events.title: Rare shark studied in its natural habitat for first time - and it wasn't where researchers thought
events.description: A rare, deep-sea goblin shark has been studied alive in its habitat for the first time.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Rare shark studied in its natural habitat for first time - and it wasn't where researchers thought

events.title: Thai princess dies aged 47
events.description: Thailand's Princess Bajrakitiyabha has died after nearly four years in a coma, aged 47.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Thai princess dies aged 47

events.title: Italy’s Meloni faces a far-right dilemma as 'Il Generale' Vannacci rises
events.description: Roberto Vannacci, a former Italian army general, is shaking up Italy's political scene with his new party, Futuro Nazionale

events.title: ‘Spider-Man of Yemen’ dies in fall while climbing volcanic crater
events.description: A Yemeni adventurer, known as “The Spider-Man of Yemen,” has died after falling into a volcano crater, authorities said
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ ‘Spider-Man of Yemen’ dies in fall while climbing volcanic crater

events.title: Britain detains sanctioned oil tanker believed to be linked to Russia’s shadow fleet
events.description: Britain’s armed forces boarded and detained a sanctioned tanker, the Smyrtos, in the English Channel on Sunday

events.title: Tens of thousands join Pride marches in Romania, Bulgaria to call for equality
events.description: Tens of thousands of LGBTQ+ supporters march in Romania and Bulgaria for annual pride parades

events.title: Thousands march in Rome in anti- and pro-migration rallies
events.description: Tens of thousands of people marched in Rome in rival demonstrations over migration

events.title: Hundreds march in Kathmandu for Nepal's Pride Month
events.description: Hundreds of members of Nepal's LGBTQ+ community and their supporters have taken to the streets of Kathmandu to celebrate Pride Month

events.title: ‘The day never stops’ for aid workers braving missiles and drones in Ukraine
events.description: As Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine enters its fifth year, conditions in southern Ukraine have rapidly deteriorated. Maintaining humanitarian operations has become an increasingly complex balancing act between enabling aid delivery and managing risk.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘The day never stops’ for aid workers braving missiles and drones in Ukraine

events.title: Judge defends barring cameras from Karmelo Anthony murder trial, says it was 'an easy decision'
events.description: Judge John Roach defends barring cameras from Karmelo Anthony's murder trial, saying the decision was necessary to ensure a fair trial for all.

events.title: Florida man arrested for DUI after troopers allegedly find 34 open alcohol containers in vehicle
events.description: A Florida man was arrested on suspicion of DUI after allegedly speeding over 90 mph on I-75 in Pasco County with 34 open alcohol containers found inside.

events.title: Menendez brothers eyed $20M insurance payout after parents' murders, Jose's ex-business partner claims
events.description: Peter M. Hoffman, who hired Jose Menendez at Carolco Pictures, shares his perspective on the 1989 killings in the memoir "Karmic Winds" and doubts abuse claims.

events.title: Ohio police chief arrested in Florida after grand jury hands down 70-count child sex indictment
events.description: Ohio police chief Chad Essert faces a 70-count indictment for alleged sex crimes involving a minor, with charges carrying up to 280 years in prison.

events.title: IDF announces elimination of Hezbollah commander behind murder of 5 American soldiers
events.description: Israel eliminates senior Hezbollah commander Ali Musa Daqduq, accused of orchestrating the 2007 kidnapping and murder of five American soldiers.

events.title: Zelenskyy congratulates Trump on turning 80 in birthday call, vows more Ukraine peace talks at G7 on Tuesday
events.description: Zelenskyy congratulated Trump on turning 80 and discussed peace efforts in a phone call ahead of the G7 Summit, where the two leaders agreed to meet.

events.title: Germany pledges to build Europe's strongest army as NATO allies answer Trump pressure
events.description: Germany pledges to become Europe's strongest conventional army inside NATO, with ambassador saying Berlin is ready to assume greater defense role.

events.title: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to 30 years over North Korea drone flights
events.description: South Korean court sentenced former President Yoon Suk Yeol to 30 years for allegedly ordering drone flights over North Korea to justify martial law.

events.title: Colombian lawmakers seek suspension of Trump foe Gustavo Petro over alleged meddling in upcoming election
events.description: Colombian lawmakers consider suspending President Gustavo Petro from office amid allegations he improperly intervened in the presidential election.

events.title: EXCLUSIVE: Serbian President Vučić says support for US 'surged' under Trump, invites him to visit Belgrade
events.description: Serbian President Vučić says relations with the U.S. have transformed under Trump, citing economic cooperation and an invitation for Trump to visit.
